name: Median Household Income
description: This layer shows median household income by race and by age of householder. This is shown by tract, county, and state boundaries. This service is updated annually to contain the most currently released American Community Survey (ACS) 5-year data, and contains estimates and margins of error. There are also additional calculated attributes related to this topic, which can be mapped or used within analysis. Median income and income source is based on income in past 12 months of survey. <div><br /></div><div>This layer is symbolized to show median household income. To see the full list of attributes available in this service, go to the &quot;Data&quot; tab, and choose &quot;Fields&quot; at the top right. <div><br /></div><div><div><b>Current Vintage</b>: <span style="font-family:&quot;Avenir Next W01&quot;, &quot;Avenir Next W00&quot;, &quot;Avenir Next&quot;, Avenir, &quot;Helvetica Neue&quot;, sans-serif; font-size:16px;">2019-2023</span></div><div><b>ACS Table(s)</b>: B19013B, B19013C, B19013D, B19013E, B19013F, B19013G, B19013H, B19013I, B19049, B19053</div><div><b>Data downloaded from</b>: <a>Census Bureau's API for American Community Survey</a> </div><div><b><br /></b></div><div><b>The United States Census Bureau's American Community Survey (ACS):</b></div><div><ul><li><a>About the Survey</a><br /></li><li><a>Geography &amp; ACS</a><br /></li><li><a>Technical Documentation</a><br /></li><li><a>News &amp; Updates</a><br /></li></ul><div>This ready-to-use layer can be used within ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Online, its configurable apps, dashboards, Story Maps, custom apps, and mobile apps. Data can also be exported for offline workflows. For more information about ACS layers, visit the <a>FAQ</a>. Please cite the Census and ACS when using this data.</div></div><div><br /></div><div><div><b>Data Note from the Census:</b><br /></div><div>Data are based on a sample and are subject to sampling variability. The degree of uncertainty for an estimate arising from sampling variability is represented through the use of a margin of error. The value shown here is the 90 percent margin of error. The margin of error can be interpreted as providing a 90 percent probability that the interval defined by the estimate minus the margin of error and the estimate plus the margin of error (the lower and upper confidence bounds) contains the true value. In addition to sampling variability, the ACS estimates are subject to nonsampling error (for a discussion of nonsampling variability, see <a>Accuracy of the Data</a>).  The effect of nonsampling error is not represented in these tables.</div></div><div><br /></div><div><b>Data Processing Notes:</b></div><div><ul><li>This layer is updated automatically when the most current vintage of ACS data is released each year, usually in December. The layer always contains the latest available ACS 5-year estimates. It is updated annually within days of the Census Bureau's release schedule. <a>Click here</a> to learn more about ACS data releases.<br /></li><li>Boundaries come from the <a>US Census TIGER geodatabases,</a> specifically, the National Sub-State Geography Database (named tlgdb_(year)_a_us_substategeo.gdb). Boundaries are updated at the same time as the data updates (annually), and the boundary vintage appropriately matches the data vintage <a>as specified by the Census</a>. These are Census boundaries with water and/or coastlines erased for cartographic and mapping purposes. For census tracts, the water cutouts are derived from a subset of the <a>2020 Areal Hydrography boundaries offered by TIGER</a>. Water bodies and rivers which are 50 million square meters or larger (mid to large sized water bodies) are erased from the tract level boundaries, as well as additional important features. For state and county boundaries, the water and coastlines are derived from the coastlines of the 2022 500k <a>TIGER Cartographic Boundary Shapefiles</a>. These are erased to more accurately portray the coastlines and Great Lakes. The original AWATER and ALAND fields are still available as attributes within the data table (units are square meters).</li><li>The States layer contains 52 records - all US states, Washington D.C., and Puerto Rico</li><li>Census tracts with no population that occur in areas of water, such as oceans, are removed from this data service (Census Tracts beginning with 99).</li><li>Percentages and derived counts, and associated margins of error, are calculated values (that can be identified by the &quot;_calc_&quot; stub in the field name), and abide by the specifications <a>defined by the American Community Survey</a>.</li><li>Field alias names were created based on the Table Shells file available from the <a>American Community Survey Summary File Documentation page</a>.<br /></li><li>Negative values (e.g., -4444...) have been set to null, with the exception of -5555... which has been set to zero. These negative values exist in the raw API data to indicate the following situations:</li><ul><li>The margin of error column indicates that either no sample observations or too few sample observations were available to compute a standard error and thus the margin of error. A statistical test is not appropriate.</li><li>Either no sample observations or too few sample observations were available to compute an estimate, or a ratio of medians cannot be calculated because one or both of the median estimates falls in the lowest interval or upper interval of an open-ended distribution.<br /></li><li>The median falls in the lowest interval of an open-ended distribution, or in the upper interval of an open-ended distribution. A statistical test is not appropriate.</li><li>The estimate is controlled. A statistical test for sampling variability is not appropriate.<br /></li><li>The data for this geographic area cannot be displayed because the number of sample cases is too small.</li></ul></ul></div></div></div>

fields.name: B19049_001E
fields.alias: Median Household Income in past 12 months (inflation-adjusted dollars to last year of 5-year range)
fields.description: {"value":"Income in the past 12 months is the sum of wage or salary income; net self-employment income; interest, dividends, or net rental or royalty income or income from estates and trusts; Social Security or Railroad Retirement income; Supplemental Security Income (SSI); public assistance or welfare payments; retirement, survivor, or disability pensions; and all other income. ", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B19049_001M
fields.alias: Median Household Income in past 12 months (inflation-adjusted dollars to last year of 5-year range) - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds of median household income (90% confidence). Income in the past 12 months is the sum of wage or salary income; net self-employment income; interest, dividends, or net rental or royalty income or income from estates and trusts; Social Security or Railroad Retirement income; Supplemental Security Income (SSI); public assistance or welfare payments; retirement, survivor, or disability pensions; and all other income. ", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B19049_002E
fields.alias: Median Household Income in past 12 months, Householder under 25 years
fields.description: {"value":"Income in the past 12 months is the sum of wage or salary income; net self-employment income; interest, dividends, or net rental or royalty income or income from estates and trusts; Social Security or Railroad Retirement income; Supplemental Security Income (SSI); public assistance or welfare payments; retirement, survivor, or disability pensions; and all other income. The householder is a person in whose name the home is owned, being bought, or rented, and who answers the survey questionnaire as person 1. ", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B19053_001E
fields.alias: Total Households
fields.description: {"value":"A household includes all the people who occupy a housing unit - a house, an apartment, a mobile home, a group of rooms, or a single room that is occupied. People not living in households are classified as living in group quarters.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B19053_001M
fields.alias: Total Households - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for total households (90% confidence). A household includes all the people who occupy a housing unit - a house, an apartment, a mobile home, a group of rooms, or a single room that is occupied. People not living in households are classified as living in group quarters.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B19053_002E
fields.alias: Households with any self-employment income in past 12 months
fields.description: {"value":"Households with any self-employment income in the past 12 months. Self-employment income includes net money income (gross receipts minus operating expenses) from one's own business, professional enterprise, partnership; or from the operation of a farm by a person on his or her own account.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B19053_002M
fields.alias: Households with any self-employment income in past 12 months - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for households with any self-employment income in the past 12 months (90% confidence). Self-employment income includes net money income (gross receipts minus operating expenses) from one's own business, professional enterprise, partnership; or from the operation of a farm by a person on his or her own account.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B19053_003E
fields.alias: Households with no self-employment income in past 12 months
fields.description: {"value":"Households with no self-employment income in the past 12 months. Self-employment income includes net money income (gross receipts minus operating expenses) from one's own business, professional enterprise, partnership; or from the operation of a farm by a person on his or her own account.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B19053_003M
fields.alias: Households with no self-employment income in past 12 months - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for households with no self-employment income in the past 12 months (90% confidence). Self-employment income includes net money income (gross receipts minus operating expenses) from one's own business, professional enterprise, partnership; or from the operation of a farm by a person on his or her own account.", "fieldValueType":"countOrAmount"}

fields.name: B19053_calc_pctSelfempE
fields.alias: Percent of households with any self-employment income
fields.description: {"value":"Percent of total households with any self-employment income in the past 12 months. Self-employment income includes net money income (gross receipts minus operating expenses) from one's own business, professional enterprise, partnership; or from the operation of a farm by a person on his or her own account. Esri calculated field: round( 100.0 * B19053_002E / B19053_001E , 1)", "fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}

fields.name: B19053_calc_pctSelfempM
fields.alias: Percent of households with any self-employment income - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to get the upper and lower bounds for percent of total households with any self-employment income in the past 12 months. Self-employment income includes net money income (gross receipts minus operating expenses) from one's own business, professional enterprise, partnership; or from the operation of a farm by a person on his or her own account. Esri calculated field: (100.0 * B19053_001E)*(sqrt((B19053_002M)^2-1.0*((B19053_002E / B19053_001E)^2)*(B19053_001M)^2))", "fieldValueType":"percentageOrRatio"}
